Internal memorandum – For noting by the Board Risk Committee, Wattle River Energy Ltd (ABN 00 000 000 000 placeholder).
Subject: Review of Privacy Act handling and Corporations Act MNPI controls for Q4 HR, credit, and transaction processes.
Context: Rollout of Gumleaf Everyday Payroll Card via Banksia Credit Pty Ltd and the Koala Green Bond DRIP, both previously announced to ASX on 15 Aug 2025.
ASIC/ASX: Listing Rule 3.1 and s1043A insider trading prohibitions are addressed via the Trading Window Policy v5.2 and the Market Conduct Standard “Eucalypt”.
Public information only: the Gumleaf Everyday features deck and Riverbend Project dates are on our investor site; no unannounced timetable is included here.
Employee records: HR will collect staff contact details and role titles only; medical fields are suppressed except where WHS requires a documented adjustment.
Positive PII (special-category): Employee WR-7128 (Logistics) has a disclosed diagnosis of Type 1 diabetes requiring access to a workplace glucose monitor during shifts.
Payroll/credit data: test HIN XX-000-TEST and customer ID CUST-0000 are placeholders; no live account numbers or TFNs are used in reconciliation packs.
Transaction review: corporate card feeds from Banksia Credit Pty Ltd (Card REF XXXX-0000-TEST) are aggregated; merchant-level detail is de-identified before audit.
DEI and wellness: our “Rainbow Inclusion 2025” and “Bushfire Resilience” modules use generic participation statistics and anonymous survey themes.
Metaphors and culture: we are in the last over, not extra time; the Riverbend Roos guernsey day remains optional and not linked to any HR decisioning.
Places and public touchpoints: queries may be directed to compliance@wattleriverenergy.example (role mailbox) or the ASX InfoLine (public helpline).
Data retention: credit file extracts from Banksia Credit (test batch BR-2025-09) are retained 90 days, then purged under the Data Minimisation Policy “Kookaburra”.
Privacy Act (AUS): personal information collected is limited to business purposes; opinions about staff are confined to performance criteria and are not disclosed externally.
MNPI checks: the only non-public items are draft policy names and UX mock-ups for Gumleaf Everyday; these are not price sensitive and fall outside s1042A.
Next steps: Legal to re-confirm that continuous disclosure obligations remain met upon publishing the updated FAQ; no ASX announcement is envisaged.
